title: Retrieval Review: Readable and Responsive Typography
summary: Retrieval Review: Readable and Responsive Typography retrieves prior skills in an original changed font loading language and viewport constraints scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css font families fallbacks, css web font loading, css type scale, css line measure spacing, css text wrapping overflow, css responsive type in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed font loading language and viewport constraints artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck typography work here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the resilient typography system here. */

options.text: Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 1: a downloaded web font displays before it loads with no layout consequences.
Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 1: choose metric-compatible fallbacks, end with a generic family, and test initial, late, failed, and user-overridden font states.
Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 1: list only one brand font because browsers synthesize a similar face when it fails.

options.text: Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 3: fixed pixel font sizes provide the most consistent accessible typography.

options.text: Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 5: ellipsis is safe for any content because users understand hidden text exists.

Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 5: allow natural wrapping, add language-aware hyphenation or emergency token breaks, and keep essential text fully available without fixed-height clipping.
Changed font loading language and viewport constraints case 5: set white-space nowrap and text-overflow ellipsis on every heading to preserve card alignment.
